evidence_text: Q: What are UGC-funded RPg programmes? Do they include part-time programmes? A: All our full-time RPg programmes are UGC-funded, while part-time RPg programmes are not. Full-time RPg students without PGS are considered self-financed. Part-time and self-financed local full-time RPg students are ineligible for the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students.
source_question: What are UGC-funded RPg programmes? Do they include part-time programmes?

evidence_text: Q: I have an IANG visa. Am I eligible for the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students? A: Students with any Hong Kong visa type (including student, dependent, and IANG) or entry permit are ineligible for this Scheme.
source_question: I have an IANG visa. Am I eligible for the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students?

evidence_text: To avoid double benefit situations, should the RPg student’s tuition fee be fully waived by any other scheme, then they cannot qualify for the tuition waiver.
normalized_value: To avoid double benefit situations, should the RPg student’s tuition fee be fully waived by any other scheme, then they cannot qualify for the tuition waiver.

evidence_text: Q: My tuition fee is waived by the University. Am I eligible for the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students? A: Students with fully waived tuition fees are ineligible. However, those with partially waived fees can receive Scheme coverage for the remaining portion.
source_question: My tuition fee is waived by the University. Am I eligible for the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students?

evidence_text: Q: If I receive the Hong Kong PhD Fellowship Scheme or other scholarships, am I still eligible for the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students? A: Local recipients of the Hong Kong PhD Fellowship Scheme or other scholarships are eligible if their scholarships, awards, or stipends don't cover tuition fees.
